Engineering FAQ

14 questions reliability engineers ask before deploying ESA.

Straight answers from our diagnostics team. No jargon where it isn't needed. Data where it is.

Technology Deployment Data & Security Performance Integration

Technology

The motor's electrical supply and mechanical load are physically coupled. Every fault in the drivetrain — bearing wear, cavitation, misalignment — creates a measurable change in the electrical signature at the MCC.

SAM4 captures current and voltage signals at up to 96 kHz, extracts mechanical and electrical features through signal processing, and uses ML models trained on thousands of industrial assets to classify fault types.

The key advantage: the measurement point is at the motor control cabinet, not on the asset itself. No sensors on the machine. No scaffolding. No ATEX permits.

See the full signal-to-insight pipeline →

Mechanical faults: bearing degradation, cavitation, misalignment, imbalance, blockage and clogging, seal wear, belt and chain issues, efficiency loss.

Electrical faults: stator winding degradation, rotor bar defects, insulation breakdown, power supply asymmetry, VFD irregularities.

Process deviations: cavitation onset, pump blockage, efficiency loss below BEP.

Detection capability varies by asset type and operating load. The full detection matrix — covering pumps, fans, compressors, conveyors, and mills — is included in the Engineering Proof Pack.

Get the detection matrix →

No. ESA and vibration are complementary, not interchangeable.

Vibration gives depth on accessible assets — high-resolution bearing defect frequencies, gear mesh patterns, resonance analysis. ESA gives breadth across the fleet — covering the 30–40% of assets that vibration sensors can't reach: submerged pumps, sealed blowers, ATEX zones, remote sites.

Many customers run both. Together they close more of the monitoring gap than either method alone.

See the full ESA vs vibration comparison →

Yes. SAM4 handles most VFD topologies. The system classifies each measurement's operating state before comparing to baseline — so a change in speed or load isn't misread as a developing fault.

Some older or non-standard drives can reduce signal quality. This is assessed during the scoping phase before deployment, not after.

Any asset driven by an AC motor. The electrical signature captures faults across the entire drivetrain — motor, coupling, gearbox, and driven load.

Primary categories: pumps (submersible, booster, borehole, process), LV and MV motors, compressors, fans and blowers, conveyors (belt, screw, chain), ESPs, gearboxes and transmissions, canned motor pumps, Archimedes screw pumps.

Scale: 10,000+ assets monitored across water, steel, chemicals, airports, oil & gas, and mining. If it runs on an AC motor, SAM4 can likely monitor it.

Motor size matters less than operating load. ESA reads mechanical condition through the electrical signal — if an asset runs at near-zero load, the mechanical signature is weaker.

Rule of thumb: detection confidence is highest on assets running at >30% of rated load. SAM4 has been deployed on assets from 11 kW to medium-voltage motors at 15 kV. Suitability for specific low-power or variable-load applications is assessed during the scoping phase.

Deployment

Less than 60 minutes per asset. Zero production downtime.

CT/VT clamps are installed at the motor control cabinet — not on the asset. A cellular gateway connects via 4G/LTE. Your fleet appears in the dashboard the same day. Baseline calibration and initial health assessment are handled by Samotics.

No scaffolding. No ATEX permits. No confined space procedures. Your maintenance team can do this — no specialist contractor required.

See the full installation process →

The installation process is the same from a 5-asset pilot to a 1,000-asset fleet — only the number of shifts changes. Timeline scales linearly with asset count and available installation windows.

Example: Yorkshire Water deployed 1,300 assets across hundreds of pumping stations using the same hardware, process, and team. SAM4 scales from a 20-asset pilot to a 10,000+ asset fleet without process changes.

Yes. The MCC is in the safe zone. Installation happens at the motor control cabinet, not in the hazardous production area.

No ATEX permits. No hot work permits. No gas testing or confined space procedures. No explosion-proof sensor certification needed. This reduces cost of equipment, installation, and cabling compared to traditional CM approaches.

Data & Security

SAM4 never touches your corporate network. Data travels over cellular (4G/LTE) from the gateway to the cloud. There is no logical or physical connection between the SAM4 gateway and your WiFi, LAN, SCADA, or OT infrastructure.

Encryption: TLS 1.2+ in transit, AES-256 at rest. Mutual TLS authentication between components.

Data residency: Primary hosting in EU cloud. Configurable residency options for customers with geographic requirements.

Certifications: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, NIS2 compliant.

Data ownership: You own all raw signal data, processed analytics, and diagnostic outputs. Full data export available at any time.

Read the full security architecture →

The gateway samples electrical signals at up to 96 kHz. Local signal conditioning is performed on the gateway before compressed data transmits via 4G/LTE cellular. This sampling rate captures high-frequency features needed for fault detection across the full frequency spectrum.

Performance

Detection rate: 90.8% on core assets (pumps, fans, conveyors — 1,874 resolved incidents, January 2024 – February 2026).

False communication rate: 0.32% — 6 false communications across 1,874 resolved incidents. This includes "false communications" (correct detection, incorrect classification). We publish the methodology because enterprise buyers deserve auditable claims.

Detection rate varies by fault type. Bearing defects and stator winding faults have the highest rates. Low-load assets and newly monitored types may take 4–8 weeks for the AI to build an operational profile.

Lead time: typically weeks to months. SAM4 is not a trip-protection system for sudden catastrophic events.

SAM4 is a fully managed service, not self-serve software. Four components: hardware at the MCC, signal processing and AI analytics, expert validation by Samotics diagnostics engineers, and a monitoring dashboard.

Every AI-flagged anomaly is reviewed by a Samotics reliability engineer before it reaches your team. They confirm the fault type, assess severity, and write the recommended action. No false positive noise. No alerts to triage. Each finding includes a specific, actionable recommendation.

You also get a named customer success manager, structured training for every user role, and the ability to add new assets at any time.

Integration

SAP PM: SAM4 alerts create notifications or maintenance orders. Asset mapping links to SAP functional locations. Alert data populates order description, priority, and recommended action.

IBM Maximo: Work orders created with fault classification, asset reference, and diagnostic context. Severity levels mapped to Maximo priority codes. Supports Maximo 7.6+ and MAS.

Other CMMS: Any platform accepting REST API input can receive SAM4 alerts. Custom integrations typically configured within 1–2 weeks.

Also available: SCADA integration via REST API, email/SMS/webhook notifications, and programmatic data exports for BI tools.

See all integration options →

Still have questions? Our engineers are happy to talk through the detail.
